marketFocus: The ceasefire is technically holding but practically crumbling. Hormuz shipping at a virtual standstill — only 6 ships Thursday, 2 oil tankers. Iran charging crypto tolls and demanding Lebanon ceasefire as a precondition. Netanyahu: 'No ceasefire in Lebanon, we won't stop.' However, Trump remains optimistic ('Iran agreeing to all things they have to agree to'), and Israel-Lebanon talks are set for Tuesday in Washington. Euro Stoxx 50 futures +0.6%. DXY regaining composure after Thursday's losses.

Thursday's Core PCE came in HOT at 0.4% MoM (vs 0.3% exp) — stagflation confirmed. Final GDP Q4 unrevised at 2.3%. Unemployment Claims 219K (slight miss). JPY PPI beat strongly (0.4% MoM, 4.2% YoY — first acceleration in years), reinforcing BOJ hawkish pressure. Chinese PPI returned to positive for the first time in 3+ years — slightly supportive for AUD/NZD. Chinese CPI missed (1.0% vs 1.2% exp) — deflation risk remains.

Today's key events: USD Core CPI m/m + CPI y/y at 13:30 FFT (CRITICAL — Close Pos. Before) and CAD Employment Change + Unemployment Rate at 13:30 FFT (CRITICAL — Close Pos. Before). USD Prelim UoM Consumer Sentiment at 15:00 FFT. ECB de Guindos speaks. Moody's France + S&P UK credit ratings after market close.

USD.tone: Core PCE HOT 0.4% — Stagflation Confirmed; CPI at 13:30 FFT is the Critical Catalyst
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. Thursday's Core PCE printed 0.4% MoM (vs 0.3% exp) — the hottest reading in months and a direct confirmation of the stagflation narrative. DXY is recovering after Wednesday's ceasefire-driven -1% drop. The CPI print at 13:30 FFT is today's critical catalyst — a hot print (above 0.3% MoM or above 3.0% YoY) would cement the USD bullish case and push Fed rate-hike expectations further. FOMC Minutes confirmed hawkish hold. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: Core PCE HOT: 0.4% MoM vs 0.3% exp — stagflation confirmed, Fed hike repricing accelerates
CPI m/m + CPI y/y at 13:30 FFT — CRITICAL; hot print cements bullish case (Close Pos. Before)
DXY recovering after ceasefire-driven -1% drop — haven demand partially restored
FOMC Minutes hawkish: 'persistently higher oil could justify rate rises' — Fed Waller confirms
Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

EUR.tone: EUR/USD Rangebound Near 1.1700 — ECB de Guindos Speaks; No Directional Conviction
EUR.summary: EUR is Neutral today. EUR/USD is rangebound near 1.1700 with no fresh catalyst. ECB de Guindos speaks today — any hawkish tone could provide a short-term lift but the structural vulnerabilities (energy importer, fragmentation risk) cap the upside. German CPI Final confirmed 2.7% YoY (in line). EU Retail Sales -0.2% (in line). ECB hawkish repricing intact (45bps by year-end) but the CPI miss last week limits conviction. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: EUR/USD rangebound near 1.1700 — no directional catalyst
ECB de Guindos speaks today — watch for hawkish tone
German CPI Final confirmed 2.7% YoY (in line) — energy shock not yet filtering through
EU Retail Sales -0.2% (in line) — consumer holding up but no upside surprise
ECB hawkish repricing intact (45bps year-end) but CPI miss limits conviction

GBP.tone: GBP/USD Stalled at 1.3440 — Light UK Calendar; BOE Repricing Slightly Dovish
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. GBP/USD stalled just above 1.3440 after Wednesday's +1% ceasefire rally. UK RICS House Price Balance came in at -23% (vs -18% exp) — the worst reading in over 2 years. Halifax HPI also missed (-0.5% MoM). BOE is now pricing slightly dovish (27bps vs 30bps pre-market). Light UK calendar today — no fresh catalyst. Moody's France and S&P UK credit ratings after close are a tail risk. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: GBP/USD stalled at 1.3440 — ceasefire rally fading as Hormuz remains contested
UK RICS House Price Balance: -23% vs -18% exp — worst in 2+ years
BOE pricing slightly dovish: 27bps vs 30bps — hawkish repricing pared back
S&P UK credit rating after close — tail risk event
Light UK calendar today — no fresh directional catalyst

JPY.tone: PPI Beat Strongly — BOJ Hawkish Pressure Builds; USD/JPY Capped at 159.11
JPY.summary: JPY is Bullish today. Thursday's PPI beat strongly: 0.4% MoM (vs 0.2% exp) and 4.2% YoY (vs 3.9% exp) — the first acceleration in years, adding direct hawkish pressure on the BOJ. Finance Minister Katayama: 'prepared to take decisive action in markets.' BOJ Deputy Governor Himino: 'will take most appropriate policy to stably hit inflation target.' USD/JPY is capped at 159.11 with verbal intervention active. Lower oil prices (ceasefire narrative) are structurally positive for Japan's current account. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: PPI BEAT: 0.4% MoM vs 0.2% exp; 4.2% YoY vs 3.9% exp — first acceleration in years
BOJ hawkish pressure building — Himino and Katayama both signal readiness to act
USD/JPY capped at 159.11 — verbal intervention active, MOF watching closely
Lower oil structurally positive for Japan current account — ceasefire narrative supports
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

AUD.tone: Chinese PPI Turns Positive — Mixed Signals; No Domestic Catalyst Today
AUD.summary: AUD is Neutral today. Chinese PPI returned to positive for the first time in 3+ years (0.1% YoY vs -0.1% exp) — a slightly supportive signal for AUD via the China channel. However, Chinese CPI missed (1.0% vs 1.2% exp) — deflation risk remains. ASX 200 was dragged lower by tech and energy. No domestic Australian catalyst today. The weekly bias remains Bearish (high-beta risk-off, no domestic catalyst) but the daily picture is mixed — neutral is the appropriate call. Weekly Bearish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: Chinese PPI turned positive: 0.1% YoY vs -0.1% exp — first positive in 3+ years
Chinese CPI missed: 1.0% vs 1.2% exp — deflation risk limits AUD upside
ASX 200 dragged lower by tech and energy — risk-off headwind
No domestic Australian catalyst today — quiet calendar
Weekly Bearish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment

CAD.tone: Employment Change + Unemployment Rate at 13:30 FFT — Pre-Data Neutral; Oil Rangebound
CAD.summary: CAD is Neutral today ahead of the critical Employment Change + Unemployment Rate at 13:30 FFT. Oil is rangebound (Brent ~$98/bbl) — the ceasefire narrative is keeping a lid on the energy premium. Canada Services PMI remains in contraction (47.2). The employment data is the key catalyst — a strong beat would shift CAD to Bullish and potentially add CAD/CHF LONG to the intraday list. Pre-data neutral is the appropriate stance. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.

Oil rangebound (Brent ~$98/bbl) — ceasefire narrative limiting energy premium
Canada Services PMI still in contraction: 47.2 — services sector weak
Pre-data neutral — employment beat would shift CAD to Bullish
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment

CHF.tone: SNB Schlegel No Hawkish Surprise — USD/CHF Stabilizing; Haven Demand Fading
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. SNB Chairman Schlegel spoke Thursday at 09:00 FFT with no hawkish surprise — the SNB intervention cap narrative remains intact. USD/CHF is stabilizing after the ceasefire-driven dip. The ceasefire reduces haven demand for CHF while the USD retains its stagflation premium. CHF Unemployment Rate beat (3.1% vs 3.2% exp) was a mild positive but insufficient to shift the bias. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: SNB Schlegel no hawkish surprise — SNB intervention cap narrative intact
USD/CHF stabilizing — ceasefire reduces CHF haven premium
CHF Unemployment Rate beat: 3.1% vs 3.2% exp — mild positive, insufficient to shift bias
USD retains stagflation premium — USD preferred haven over CHF
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

NZD.tone: RBNZ Hawkish Surprise — ASB Bank Sees Two More Hikes; NZD Outperforming G10
NZD.summary: NZD is Bullish today. The RBNZ delivered a hawkish surprise Wednesday — Governor Breman: 'inflation expected to increase considerably, will act decisively if core prices pick up.' ASB Bank now sees RBNZ hiking September AND December. NZD/USD is on the 0.58 handle and has outperformed all G10 currencies for two consecutive sessions. 75bps of tightening priced by year-end (up 15bps since last week). The RBNZ narrative is the dominant driver — overriding the high-beta risk-off headwind. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment (mismatch) — intraday only.
drivers: RBNZ hawkish: Breman 'will act decisively if core prices pick up' — market pricing 75bps
ASB Bank sees RBNZ hiking September AND December — repricing accelerating
NZD/USD on 0.58 handle — outperformed all G10 for two consecutive sessions
75bps of tightening priced by year-end (up 15bps since last week)
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment — intraday only, no swing setup
